# [DefaultDict Tutorial](https://www.hackerrank.com/challenges/defaultdict-tutorial/problem?isFullScreen=true)
## Easy
<div class="challenge-body-html"><div class="challenge_problem_statement"><div class="msB challenge_problem_statement_body"><div class="hackdown-content"><svg style="display: none;"><defs id="MathJax_SVG_glyphs"></defs></svg><p>The <em>defaultdict</em> tool is a container in the collections class of Python. It's similar to the usual dictionary (<em>dict</em>) container, but the only difference is that a defaultdict will have a <em>default</em> value if that key has not been set yet. If you didn't use a defaultdict you'd have to check to see if that key exists, and if it doesn't, set it to what you want. <br>
<strong>For example:</strong></p>

<pre><code>from collections import defaultdict
d = defaultdict(list)
d['python'].append("awesome")
d['something-else'].append("not relevant")
d['python'].append("language")
for i in d.items():
    print i
</code></pre>

<p>This prints:</p>

<pre><code>('python', ['awesome', 'language'])
('something-else', ['not relevant'])
</code></pre>

<p><strong>Example</strong>  </p>

<p>Group A contains 'a', 'b', 'a'
Group B contains 'a', 'c'</p>
